full_text:
TLS meeting

Ada, Hanna, Zhihan
11.11.2025

#1 - H&E seg and low-plex CycIF

Hanna

Milky spots

>100 MS in human omentum, 300-700 um diameter

induce inflammation but lack DCs → no antigen presenting capability

unlike conventional lymphoid organs, develop in the absence of LTi

primary colonization site for tumor cells in omentum → can be later replaced with tumor mass

Fibroblastic reticular cells (FRC) are specialized lymphoid organ fibroblasts surrounding and supporting MS, Lagg, and TLS (increasing expression MS → Lagg → iTLS → mTLS)

Milky spots vs. Lagg

Lagg develop in response during inflammation but also MS may activate in some level

less HEVs: ICs in Lagg are recruited via HEVs
lower IL-6 expression
less proliferating cells (Ki-67)
less abundant and less active fibroblastic reticular cell (FRC) network around MS (=lower PDPN expression)
more or less M1/M2 macrophages? M1 count increases in the beginning of inflammation but M2 more prevalent in chronic inflammation and cancer

Questions from low-plex tCycIF: ben/bord omentum samples vs. malignant PDS omentum samples

Descriptive analysis of MS and Lagg
Can we distinguish MS from Lagg from PDS omentum cases? diameter, cell density, cell composition (CD4/CD8, M1/M2, PD-1, Ki-67)
How many “Lagg” in malignant samples are actually MS
Are some ben/bord aggregates Lagg rather than MS

Low-plex CycIF panel

Main aims:
MS+ ben/bord omentums 
Omentum tumors with Laggs located in stroma, (interface), and intratumor 
QUESTIONS:
Are MS different from Lagg? → in MS, macrophages predominant cell type
Are some ben/bord aggregates Lagg rather than milky spots?
- compare HRD HRP PDS samples
Potential sample count:
MS+ ben/bord omentum: 9 tumors (3 benign + 6 borderline)
PDS-treated, omentum tumors with at least one Lagg in all three sites (stroma, intratumor, and TSI): 8 tumors

Sample selection for low-plex CycIF

All Lagg+/TLS+ benign (N=3) and borderline (N=6) tumors
Lagg+ omentum tumors, PDS-treated, Laggs located in intratumor, stroma, and interface 
Lagg+TLS+ tumors and Lagg+TLS- tumors, PDS treated
both adnex and omentum tumors, randomly
both adnex and omentum tumors, match patients if exist

PDS-treated omentum tumors with at least 2 Lagg: N=58 - Of these, 8 tumors have Lagg in all 3 sites

Samples to CycIF: Milky spots vs. Lagg

MS+ ben/bord omentum vs. Lagg+ omentum (both PDS)
MS+ ben/bord omentums 
Omentum tumors with Laggs located in stroma, (interface), and intratumor 
QUESTIONS:
Are MS different from Lagg? → in MS, macrophages predominant cell type
Are some ben/bord aggregates Lagg rather than milky spots?
- compare HRD HRP PDS samples
Potential sample count:
MS+ ben/bord omentum: 9 tumors (3 benign + 6 borderline)
PDS-treated, omentum tumors with at least one Lagg in all three sites (stroma, intratumor, and TSI): 8 tumors

Samples to CycIF: TLS+ vs. TLS- tumors

Lagg+/TLS+ tumors vs. Lagg+/TLS- tumors (only PDS/PDS+IDS/ome, adn or both?)
Lagg+ TLS+ tumors
Lagg+ TLS- tumors 
QUESTIONS:
Differences between Lagg metrics/cell composition between TLS+ and TLS- tumors? → can we identify Lagg that potentially develop into TLS?

Potential sample count:
Lagg+/TLS+: 69 tumors (of which 29 PDS, 39 IDS, 1 NA // 4 adnex, 63 omentum)
Lagg+/TLS-: 132 tumors (of which 69 PDS, 61 IDS // 35 adnex, 92 omentum)

#2 - GeoMx & tCycIF

Zhihan, Ada

MS up score / MS down score

ms_down_genes <- c("BCL6","MKI67","TOP2A","STMN1","PDCD1", "PDPN", "IL6", "ST6GAL1")

ms_up_genes <- c("CD163","MRC1","FABP4","APOL1","IL10RA")

Scatter plot of key genes for LAgg

BCR profiling - SCellex

Main aims:
Verify the feasibility of the SCellex platform for BCR (and optional TCR) on our samples in FF and FFPE. 
STEPS:
Test in the tubes/whole FFPE 
whether SCellex's BCR/TCR amplification can work (gene-specific / random amplification) 
compared with Bulk BCR results
Test on regional FFPE tissue and the spotting regional barcode 
whether barcode beads on tissue spots can preserve spatial information and reconstruct BCR/TCR
FF samples for selected samples
TODO:
Sample selection for FFPE and sectioning + FF samples
2 tube - 1 with single scraped sample / 1 with multi scraped samples

BCR profiling - MiXCR for Bulk RNAseq

Main aims:
The BCR sequence was extracted and reconstructed from the existing Bulk RNA-seq data on the iCAN platform using the MiXCR software to identify clonal and immune diversity, and compared with morphology and expression data (B cell enrichment level, TLS group, etc.).
Compare MiXCR results with deconvolution results from Kassandra & PRISM. 
STEPS:
Cohort selection and prepare Bulk RNAseq data in personal folder
Run the MiXCR pipeline to perform BCR sequence assembly and clonotype calling
QC for MiXCR results
Use the R package immunarch for downstream visualization and statistical analysis
Compare with deconvolution results

GeoMx - Biological questions and next steps

Main questions:
TLS functionality
Is the emergence/maturation of TLS associated with antitumor immunity?
Do the distributions of immunosuppressive markers and activation markers differ in TLS at different maturity levels?
TLS maturation axis
How can the continuous spectrum of TLS from basic to mature be quantified?
Recruitment / positioning mechanism of TLS
How are TLS B/T cell recruitment and spatial positioning driven by chemokine?
Cell interactions and spatial mechanisms of TLS
How do B/T cell colocalization patterns change with TLS maturity?
Do specific cell-cell interaction patterns exist?
Clonality and function
TLS in TME
Does TLS drive the distribution or functional state of immune cells in the tumor microenvironment?
Treatment relevance & predictive markers

Next steps for GeoMx analysis

Normalization + Remove Batch effect for only TLS AOIs
Check quality issues / re-QC / resequencing to recover more TLS AOIs data
Check those relabeled AOIs in the image and see if the new/old labels match with the morphology
R script is uploaded to Github/TLS/GeoMx
a supplementary table for gene signature
New TLS labels based on GC signature
ROIs alignment with tcycif cell counts
Differential gene expression / Enrichment analysis
Find and check interesting TLS related pathways, key genes / related functions inside
Deconvolution analysis - TLS cell franctions distributions
Spatial pattern recognition
Cell-cell interactions (ligand–receptor analysis)
Any more ideas?
